message:
feat(harness): track orphan slot reclamation in cross-process crash recovery

In the crash recovery path of execute_cycle(), the slot was being
unconditionally cleared to None without first checking whether it
actually held an active value. This meant orphan slot reclamations --
slots that were occupied when the simulated crash occurred -- were
silently discarded without being counted in metrics.

This change adds a guard before the slot clear that:
1. Checks if the slot at slot_idx is Some (i.e., was occupied at crash
   time), indicating it is an orphan that needs reclamation.
2. Increments metrics.orphan_slots_reclaimed_total to track how many
   orphan slots have been reclaimed across all crash recovery cycles.
3. Sets the slot_reclaimed flag to true so downstream logic can
   distinguish cycles that performed reclamation from those that did
   not.

The slot is still cleared to None immediately after, preserving the
existing recovery semantics. This is purely additive instrumentation
for better observability of the crash harness behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
